[2018-05-07 23:17] elyot: @mike We realized that supporter tiers don't have campaign access ATM. We will be fixing it within 24h. My apologies.
[2018-05-07 23:25] elyot: @theovermaster Animation speed settings do not effect how fast the AI thinks or clicks, and those two missions use our AI. We can make it affect the AI click speed though, I think we should maybe.
[2018-05-07 23:31] elyot: @Leary93 Think of it this way... suppose p1 had 7 drones and p2 had 8... would that be similarly balanced to the current 6 vs 7? Well it's the same thing, so yes. The idea is that 7 is halfway between 6 and 8 and taking a gold away from p2 would move it closer to 6 which would disadvantage p2. We can measure the "inflation" or "growth rate" of units and try to give p2 a starting situation which is halfway between the "turn 1 value" and "turn 2 value" of what p1 has, and that's pretty much what we did.

messages.author: elyot

messages.author: elyot
messages.content: @theovermaster Animation speed settings do not effect how fast the AI thinks or clicks, and those two missions use our AI. We can make it affect the AI click speed though, I think we should maybe.

messages.author: elyot
messages.content: @Leary93 Think of it this way... suppose p1 had 7 drones and p2 had 8... would that be similarly balanced to the current 6 vs 7? Well it's the same thing, so yes. The idea is that 7 is halfway between 6 and 8 and taking a gold away from p2 would move it closer to 6 which would disadvantage p2. [2018-05-13 18:32] elyot: ty I'll update it, actually I have a few more infopanel fixes to do first (kill some widows etc.)
[2018-05-13 18:34] elyot: @oceanbrz Years of grinding balance basically. Not much else. We had a knob to adjust, which is green's HP, and we increased it until it balanced blue's absorb and red's attack. We knew that we wanted high cost on green so it was fine. We really wanted R/G/B to feel as different as possible and not be symmetric like the land in magic, to encourage diversity of builds based on p1/p2 opening combinatorics

[2018-05-21 02:09] elyot: @RiFT (#RIPBrowserers) consuming doesn't work with units that have multiple isomorphism classes without targeting, like even consuming a gauss cannon would require you to pick which one (or infer it) because of different HP. There are a number of reasons for this, and in theory they can be worked around, but in practice, they help keep a clean understanding of what the gamestate is
[2018-05-21 02:10] crash_overlord: Shouldn't it always pick lowest HP because of SDR anyways? Or are you going to move away from SDR in the future?
[2018-05-21 02:11] elyot: Well it's more like... say we want to have a thing that targets a green unit and heals it
[2018-05-21 02:11] elyot: does that commute with sacrificing? how does it behave?
[2018-05-21 02:12] elyot: Like there are a lot of intricacies with how undo/unclick works with freezing, sniping, frontline, and breaching, but it's all resolved through some core principles of priority etc.
[2018-05-21 02:12] elyot: whenever we ran into one of these awkward design decisions, like "how does fragile + frontline behave", we just punted on it
[2018-05-21 02:12] elyot: cuz we didn't need more design space at the time
[2018-05-21 02:13] elyot: and sacrificing prompt units is similar
[2018-05-21 02:13] elyot: we just never programmed it to work yet, but we could, if we decided how we wanted it to work
[2018-05-21 02:14] elyot: and then checked a lot of consequences and proved a few theorems that establish that unclick/reordering can't make things go wrong
[2018-05-21 02:14] elyot: like it's not a problem for simple cases, but you have to imagine designing literally any prismata unit with that mechanic plus others
[2018-05-21 02:15] elyot: @f0x same with your question, basically we never made them yet... so... 😉
